metadata.name: HotReload
metadata.description: Essay: how hot reload works on each platform — Dynamic mode on iOS, Dynamic mode on Android, Next.js dev on web — and why one edit yields three live reloads. Four H2 sections. ~5-min read.

dataFlow.diagram:
flowchart TD
    VIEW[HotReloadView] --> VM[HotReloadViewModel]
    VM -- nextReadLinks --> VIEW

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_viewmodel -> /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state, next_why_spec_first -> /concepts/why-spec-first) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the concepts_hot_reload_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped concept URLs enumerated in transitions: /concepts, /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state, /concepts/why-spec-first.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state and /concepts/why-spec-first. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

transitions.condition: url is a spec-mapped concept URL or /concepts

notes: Fifth and final concepts essay. Flipping CONCEPTS_ENTRIES row 5 to 'live' completes the Concepts tab.
Focus on the *mechanism*, not the UX — this page is for readers who want to understand why changing a JSON in an editor causes their iPhone simulator to re-render.
